title: salix × paracalliantha
intro: Salix × paracalliantha, a hybrid willow tree, is a cross between Salix alba and Salix fragilis, characterized by its distinctive white or pale yellow flowers and slender branches. The bark of Salix × paracalliantha has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to help with wound healing and reducing pain. The plant is also said to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving rheumatic pains, and acting as a natural diuretic. Salix × paracalliantha is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically to the skin or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
